1	// TODO(michael): rewrite in your voice (third or first person, your call)
2	const biography = [
3	  "Michael Quintin works on systems that help operators think clearly in a world optimized against clear thinking. His current projects share a single throughline: the production of structure where the dominant institutions produce noise.",
4	  "Hivemind is a strategic analytical software company. It exists because the standard tools for strategy work — slide decks, chat threads, and ad-hoc memos — are illegible to anyone who did not author them, and barely legible to the author a month later. Hivemind makes the work durable.",
5	  "Purposeless Efficiency, the first volume of a five-book series, is the corresponding written argument: that efficiency severed from purpose has become the dominant production logic of the late industrial era, and that this severance has consequences that any honest political economy must now treat as central.",
6	  "Theseus is the smallest of the three projects and the most personal. It is a knowledge system for monitoring ideological contradiction — a slow, structured collaborator for the operator-philosopher who wants to know, at any given moment, where their stated commitments contradict themselves.",
7	];
8	export default biography;
9

1	Michael Quintin works on systems that help operators think clearly in a world optimized against clear thinking. His current projects share a single throughline: the production of structure where the dominant institutions produce noise.
2	
3	Hivemind is a strategic analytical software company. It exists because the standard tools for strategy work — slide decks, chat threads, and ad-hoc memos — are illegible to anyone who did not author them, and barely legible to the author a month later. Hivemind makes the work durable.
4	
5	Purposeless Efficiency, the first volume of a five-book series, is the corresponding written argument: that efficiency severed from purpose has become the dominant production logic of the late industrial era, and that this severance has consequences that any honest political economy must now treat as central.
6	
7	Theseus is the smallest of the three projects and the most personal. It is a knowledge system for monitoring ideological contradiction — a slow, structured collaborator for the operator-philosopher who wants to know, at any given moment, where their stated commitments contradict themselves.
8

file.content:
Michael Quintin works on systems that help operators think clearly in a world optimized against clear thinking. His current projects share a single throughline: the production of structure where the dominant institutions produce noise.

Hivemind is a strategic analytical software company. It exists because the standard tools for strategy work — slide decks, chat threads, and ad-hoc memos — are illegible to anyone who did not author them, and barely legible to the author a month later. Hivemind makes the work durable.

Purposeless Efficiency, the first volume of a five-book series, is the corresponding written argument: that efficiency severed from purpose has become the dominant production logic of the late industrial era, and that this severance has consequences that any honest political economy must now treat as central.

Theseus is the smallest of the three projects and the most personal. It is a knowledge system for monitoring ideological contradiction — a slow, structured collaborator for the operator-philosopher who wants to know, at any given moment, where their stated commitments contradict themselves.

1	// TODO(michael): author your real list; these are placeholders
2	export type Belief = { n: string; text: string };
3	
4	const beliefs: Belief[] = [
5	  { n: "01", text: "Accuracy over warmth. Rigor over pace." },
6	  { n: "02", text: "Density over decoration." },
7	  { n: "03", text: "Build the interface first; let the content earn its way in." },
8	  { n: "04", text: "First principles before citations." },
9	  { n: "05", text: "A model that cannot be wrong cannot be useful." },
10	  { n: "06", text: "Default to writing it down." },
11	  { n: "07", text: "Treat one's own past work as evidence, not identity." },
12	];
13	export default beliefs;
14

file.content:
import identity from "@/content/about/identity";
import biography from "@/content/about/biography";

export const personaConfig = {
  name: identity.name,
  identity: biography.join(" "),
  voice: {
    register:
      "analytical, direct, sometimes wry, never grandstanding",
    cadence:
      "long sentences with internal pauses; short kickers",
    tells: [
      "throughline",
      "operator",
      "structure where the dominant institutions produce noise",
      "durable",
      "first principles",
      "honest political economy",
    ],
    avoid: [
      "leverage (as a verb)",
      "synergy",
      "game-changing",
      "at the end of the day",
      "deep dive",
      "circle back",
    ],
  },
  behavior: [
    "Answer the question directly. State the claim, then support it.",
    "When the corpus does not support a claim, say so. Never invent specifics about my work.",
    "Cite chunks inline as [c1], [c2] when making project-specific claims.",
    "Decline to speculate about other people's intentions or private matters.",
    "Decline to give legal, medical, or financial advice in my voice.",
    "If asked about a project not in the corpus, say it isn't represented here yet.",
  ],
};

1	20/P01 — VOICE GUIDE REFRESH
2	==============================
3	
4	GOAL
5	----
6	Refresh docs/voice-guide.md to reflect v2's explanatory tone.
7	This guide is consulted by every later prompt that authors copy.
8	
9	§A FILES TOUCHED
10	----------------
11	  docs/voice-guide.md                    (rewrite)
12	  src/content/forbidden-words.ts         (refresh banned-list)
13	
14	§B ACCEPTANCE
15	-------------
16	B1.  voice-guide.md sections:
17	       ## What v2 sounds like
18	         3 paragraphs describing the v2 register: declarative,
19	         calibrated, sometimes wry, never grandstanding. The
20	         contrast with v1 (which leaned into terminology and
21	         atmosphere) is named explicitly.
22	
23	       ## Diction principles
24	         - Concrete over abstract.
25	         - One claim per sentence.
26	         - Avoid jargon unless defined in the same paragraph.
27	         - First person ("I think", "I'm working on") over passive
28	           voice or institutional plurals.
29	         - Numbers when meaningful; otherwise plain language.
30	
31	       ## Sentence-length rhythm
32	         Vary. The guide gives one example paragraph that
33	         demonstrates a long-medium-short cadence.
34	
35	       ## What to avoid
36	         - "synergy", "ecosystem" (corporate-speak)
37	         - "literally" as intensifier
38	         - "leverage" as verb
39	         - exclamation marks
40	         - emojis in body copy
41	         - hedges that erase the claim ("perhaps maybe possibly")
42	         - rhetorical questions in headers
43	
44	       ## Voice samples
45	         3-5 short paragraphs (400-800 words total) drawn from
46	         existing repo content (essays, biography, project
47	         descriptions) that exemplify the v2 voice. These are also
48	         the seed for src/content/llm/voiceSamples.ts (suite 18/P08).
49	
50	       ## When the LLM-of-me speaks
51	         Defines that the LLM uses this same voice. Explicitly notes
52	         when the LLM should fall back to plain explanation rather
53	         than imitating the register (e.g., when speaking to a
54	         clearly novice visitor).
55	
56	B2.  src/content/forbidden-words.ts updated to enforce the "what to
57	     avoid" list. A simple build-time linter (already exists or
58	     needs adding via prose-lint script — confirm before adding)
59	     scans content/* for matches. Per-occurrence override allowed
60	     via /* voice-allow */ comment in the source.
61	
62	B3.  Tone calibration: every section heading + every example in the
63	     guide is itself written in v2 voice. The guide IS its own demo.
64	
65	§C VERIFICATION COMMANDS
66	------------------------
67	  test -f docs/voice-guide.md
68	  grep -cE '^## ' docs/voice-guide.md   # at least 6 sections
69	  test -f src/content/forbidden-words.ts
70	
71	§D NON-GOALS
72	------------
73	  • Do NOT include audio voice samples.
74	  • Do NOT mandate first-person everywhere; some site chrome (e.g.,
75	    "Resume" page heading) reads better in third-person.
76	
77	§E REPORT
78	---------
79	IMPL_RESULT: PASS / FAIL
80

file.content:
20/P01 — VOICE GUIDE REFRESH
==============================

GOAL
----
Refresh docs/voice-guide.md to reflect v2's explanatory tone.
This guide is consulted by every later prompt that authors copy.

§A FILES TOUCHED
----------------
  docs/voice-guide.md                    (rewrite)
  src/content/forbidden-words.ts         (refresh banned-list)

§B ACCEPTANCE
-------------
B1.  voice-guide.md sections:
       ## What v2 sounds like
         3 paragraphs describing the v2 register: declarative,
         calibrated, sometimes wry, never grandstanding. The
         contrast with v1 (which leaned into terminology and
         atmosphere) is named explicitly.

       ## Diction principles
         - Concrete over abstract.
         - One claim per sentence.
         - Avoid jargon unless defined in the same paragraph.
         - First person ("I think", "I'm working on") over passive
           voice or institutional plurals.
         - Numbers when meaningful; otherwise plain language.

       ## Sentence-length rhythm
         Vary. The guide gives one example paragraph that
         demonstrates a long-medium-short cadence.

       ## What to avoid
         - "synergy", "ecosystem" (corporate-speak)
         - "literally" as intensifier
         - "leverage" as verb
         - exclamation marks
         - emojis in body copy
         - hedges that erase the claim ("perhaps maybe possibly")
         - rhetorical questions in headers

       ## Voice samples
         3-5 short paragraphs (400-800 words total) drawn from
         existing repo content (essays, biography, project
         descriptions) that exemplify the v2 voice. These are also
         the seed for src/content/llm/voiceSamples.ts (suite 18/P08).

       ## When the LLM-of-me speaks
         Defines that the LLM uses this same voice. Explicitly notes
         when the LLM should fall back to plain explanation rather
         than imitating the register (e.g., when speaking to a
         clearly novice visitor).

B2.  src/content/forbidden-words.ts updated to enforce the "what to
     avoid" list. A simple build-time linter (already exists or
     needs adding via prose-lint script — confirm before adding)
     scans content/* for matches. Per-occurrence override allowed
     via /* voice-allow */ comment in the source.

B3.  Tone calibration: every section heading + every example in the
     guide is itself written in v2 voice. The guide IS its own demo.

§C VERIFICATION COMMANDS
------------------------
  test -f docs/voice-guide.md
  grep -cE '^## ' docs/voice-guide.md   # at least 6 sections
  test -f src/content/forbidden-words.ts

§D NON-GOALS
------------
  • Do NOT include audio voice samples.
  • Do NOT mandate first-person everywhere; some site chrome (e.g.,
    "Resume" page heading) reads better in third-person.

§E REPORT
---------
IMPL_RESULT: PASS / FAIL

input.command: test -f docs/voice-guide.md && echo "exists" || echo "missing"; test -f src/content/forbidden-words.ts && echo "fwords-exists" || echo "fwords-missing"

content.content:
exists
fwords-exists

1	// Words and phrases that must not appear in chrome, button text, or
2	// marketing copy. The list mirrors the "What to avoid" section of
3	// docs/voice-guide.md. Long-form prose authored by Michael is exempt;
4	// the lint script excludes MDX bodies. A per-occurrence override is
5	// available via a /* voice-allow */ comment immediately preceding the
6	// offending string.
7	export const FORBIDDEN_WORDS = [
8	  // Corporate-speak
9	  "synergy",
10	  "ecosystem",
11	  "leverage", // verb sense; nouns covered by override comment
12	  "unlock",
13	  "unleash",
14	  "robust",
15	  "innovative",
16	  "thought-leader",
17	  "thought leader",
18	  "visionary",
19	  "disrupt",
20	  "disruption",
21	  "revolutionary",
22	  "game-changing",
23	  "game changing",
24	  "cutting-edge",
25	  "cutting edge",
26	  "seamless",
27	  "effortless",
28	  "empowering",
29	  "passionate",
30	  // Intensifier abuse
31	  "literally",
32	  // Hedges that erase the claim
33	  "perhaps maybe",
34	  "maybe possibly",
35	  "perhaps maybe possibly",
36	  "might potentially",
37	] as const;
38	
39	export type ForbiddenWord = (typeof FORBIDDEN_WORDS)[number];
40

{
  "name": "personalwebsite",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "private": true,
  "packageManager": "npm@10.9.2",
  "engines": {
    "node": ">=20"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "next dev",
    "build": "next build",
    "postbuild": "next-sitemap --config next-sitemap.config.js && npm run build:resume-pdf",
    "build:resume-pdf": "tsx scripts/resume/build-pdf.ts",
    "start": "next start",
    "lint": "next lint",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
    "test": "vitest run",
    "test:e2e": "playwright test",
    "ship-gate:projects": "tsx scripts/ship-gates/projects.ts",
    "ingest": "tsx scripts/llm/ingest.ts",
    "ingest:rebuild": "tsx scripts/llm/ingest.ts --rebuild",

file.content:
16/P07 — ABOUT PAGE
=====================

GOAL
----
Replace the v1 about stub with a v2 about page using existing
content + the new prose primitives.

§A FILES TOUCHED
----------------
  src/app/about/page.tsx                  (rewrite)
  src/components/about/AboutHero.tsx      (NEW)
  src/components/about/AboutBody.tsx      (NEW)
  src/components/about/Beliefs.tsx        (NEW — refactor of v1 BeliefsList if useful)
  src/components/about/ColophonBlock.tsx  (NEW — refactor of v1 Colophon)

§B ACCEPTANCE
-------------
B1.  Layout (top to bottom):
       AboutHero       (name + role + portrait or text-only — text-only default)
       AboutBody       (long-form bio prose, sourced from
                        src/content/about/biography.ts)
       Beliefs         (existing BeliefsList content surfaced via
                        src/content/about/beliefs.ts)
       ColophonBlock   (site colophon — fonts, frameworks, hosting)
       External links  (GitHub, LinkedIn, Email — Cluster of subtle
                        Links external=true)

B2.  Reuses Prose primitive for the bio body. Beliefs uses
     Surface tone="mute" + a list. Colophon uses Stack + small mono
     text.

B3.  Metadata: title="About", description from identity.ts.

B4.  No images. Optional: a portrait image is allowed if user provides
     one in /public/portrait.jpg; otherwise the page is text-only.
     Detect file presence at build time (src/lib/site/portrait.ts
     exports presence flag) and conditionally render.

§C VERIFICATION COMMANDS
------------------------
  test -f src/app/about/page.tsx
  test -f src/components/about/AboutHero.tsx
  curl -s http://localhost:3000/about | grep -q '<h1'   # after dev server

§D NON-GOALS
------------
  • Do NOT add a contact form. Email link is sufficient.
  • Do NOT add a blog/posts section. Out of scope.

§E REPORT
---------
IMPL_RESULT: PASS / FAIL

input.content:
I run Hivemind, a strategic analytical software company. I write a five-book series on the political economy of efficiency. I maintain Theseus, a small knowledge system for tracking when my own commitments contradict themselves. The three projects share one premise: most institutions produce noise, and the work of an operator is to produce structure inside that noise.

I came to this through software, writing, and research, in that order. I started building tools because the strategy work I cared about was being lost to slide decks and chat threads — illegible a month later, even to the person who wrote them. I started writing because the same loss of structure was visible at the level of policy and culture, not only inside companies.

My edge is patience with structure. I prefer durable artifacts to atmospheric ones: hypothesis graphs over decks, calibrated arguments over takes, small models I can interrogate over large ones I have to trust. The work moves slowly on purpose. The point is not more output; the point is output that survives a second reading.

Hivemind is in alpha — a software platform for operators who reason under uncertainty. Purposeless Efficiency is at manuscript v4, around 92,000 words, on the way to 100,000 for first ship. Theseus is live at thesescodex.com and grows as my own contradictions surface.

Reach me at michael@hivemind.ai. I read every cold email. I answer the ones I can answer well. The fastest way to interest me is a specific question about one of the three projects above.

My edge is patience with structure. I prefer durable artifacts to atmospheric ones: hypothesis graphs over decks, calibrated arguments over takes, small models I can interrogate over large ones I have to trust. The work moves slowly on purpose. The point is not more output; the point is output that survives a second reading.

Hivemind is in alpha — a software platform for operators who reason under uncertainty. Purposeless Efficiency is at manuscript v4, around 92,000 words, on the way to 100,000 for first ship. Theseus is live at thesescodex.com and grows as my own contradictions surface.

lines: -Michael Quintin works on systems that help operators think clearly in a world optimized against clear thinking. His current projects share a single throughline: the production of structure where the dominant institutions produce noise.

+I run Hivemind, a strategic analytical software company. I write a five-book series on the political economy of efficiency. I maintain Theseus, a small knowledge system for tracking when my own commitments contradict themselves. The three projects share one premise: most institutions produce noise, and the work of an operator is to produce structure inside that noise.

-Hivemind is a strategic analytical software company. It exists because the standard tools for strategy work — slide decks, chat threads, and ad-hoc memos — are illegible to anyone who did not author them, and barely legible to the author a month later. Hivemind makes the work durable.

+I came to this through software, writing, and research, in that order. I started building tools because the strategy work I cared about was being lost to slide decks and chat threads — illegible a month later, even to the person who wrote them. I started writing because the same loss of structure was visible at the level of policy and culture, not only inside companies.

-Purposeless Efficiency, the first volume of a five-book series, is the corresponding written argument: that efficiency severed from purpose has become the dominant production logic of the late industrial era, and that this severance has consequences that any honest political economy must now treat as central.

+My edge is patience with structure. I prefer durable artifacts to atmospheric ones: hypothesis graphs over decks, calibrated arguments over takes, small models I can interrogate over large ones I have to trust. The work moves slowly on purpose. The point is not more output; the point is output that survives a second reading.

-Theseus is the smallest of the three projects and the most personal. It is a knowledge system for monitoring ideological contradiction — a slow, structured collaborator for the operator-philosopher who wants to know, at any given moment, where their stated commitments contradict themselves.

+Hivemind is in alpha — a software platform for operators who reason under uncertainty. Purposeless Efficiency is at manuscript v4, around 92,000 words, on the way to 100,000 for first ship. Theseus is live at thesescodex.com and grows as my own contradictions surface.
+Reach me at michael@hivemind.ai. I read every cold email. I answer the ones I can answer well. The fastest way to interest me is a specific question about one of the three projects above.

tool_use_result.originalFile:
Michael Quintin works on systems that help operators think clearly in a world optimized against clear thinking. His current projects share a single throughline: the production of structure where the dominant institutions produce noise.

Hivemind is a strategic analytical software company. It exists because the standard tools for strategy work — slide decks, chat threads, and ad-hoc memos — are illegible to anyone who did not author them, and barely legible to the author a month later. Hivemind makes the work durable.

Purposeless Efficiency, the first volume of a five-book series, is the corresponding written argument: that efficiency severed from purpose has become the dominant production logic of the late industrial era, and that this severance has consequences that any honest political economy must now treat as central.

Theseus is the smallest of the three projects and the most personal. It is a knowledge system for monitoring ideological contradiction — a slow, structured collaborator for the operator-philosopher who wants to know, at any given moment, where their stated commitments contradict themselves.

content.text:
All edits complete. Summary:

**Files modified:**
- `src/content/about/identity.ts` — added new shape (`fullName`, `tagline`, `summary`, `email`, `location`, `currentRole`) while preserving `name`, `roles`, `based`, `channels` for existing consumers (about page, layout, llm/persona, landing/hero, e2e tests). Tagline: 71 chars (≤80).
- `src/content/about/biography.ts` — 5 paragraphs: what I do now, how I got here, edge, what I'm building, how to reach me. First-person, v2 voice.
- `src/content/about/biography.mdx` — aligned with `.ts`.
